title: Aureline filesystem-identity, save-target-token, and semantic-readiness vocabulary
description: Cross-surface vocabulary schema for the filesystem-identity layers (presentation_path, logical_workspace_identity, canonical_filesystem_object, alias_set, save_target_token) and the semantic-readiness state set frozen in docs/filesystem/filesystem_identity_vocabulary.md. The ADR of record for the underlying filesystem-identity, watcher, save-pipeline, root-capability, and cache-identity rules is docs/adr/0006-vfs-save-cache-identity.md; the VFS crate Rust types are the schema of record and this file is the export boundary every non-VFS surface (product, CLI, support/export, graph, search, AI apply, review, mutation-journal, replay) reads. This schema freezes the names each surface uses when it renders, logs, exports, or explains the same object; it reserves projection fields for readiness chips, not-ready explainers, safe-next-action rows, and support/export parity so later M0 conformance packets do not invent parallel readiness dialects. Adding a new semantic_readiness_state, not_ready_reason, safe_next_action, or projection slot is additive-minor; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

filesystem_identity_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this filesystem-identity vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

uri.description: Layer 1 of the filesystem-identity model: the URI the user opened. Preserved verbatim by tabs, breadcrumbs, copy/paste, CLI output, and support bundle entries; never silently rewritten to the canonical URI.

display_label.description: Human-readable label. Surface chrome shows this verbatim; the label is never inferred from the canonical path when the presentation path was opened under a different string.

root_badge.description: Short chip labelling the root the path belongs to (local, remote, container, virtual, archive). Surface chrome shows this verbatim.

presentation_path.description: Layer 1: the path the user opened. Carried alongside layers 2-5 on every file-open, rename, save, autosave, compare, restore, external-change, and AI / apply flow.

workspace_id.description: Stable workspace identifier. Survives rename, alias change, and canonical-path renormalisation.

root_id.description: Stable identifier for the workspace root the object lives under.

logical_uri.description: Workspace-relative logical URI. Search, history, AI, review, and CLI address one workspace object through this URI regardless of the visible path or alias.

trust_state.description: Workspace trust posture at the time the record was emitted.

policy_scope.description: Optional policy scope the workspace is attached to (managed overlay, tenant policy bundle, compliance scope). Surfaces cite this when a policy blocks save or rename.

logical_workspace_identity.description: Layer 2: the workspace object the product is tracking.

canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI after symlink / junction / alias resolution.

normalization_form.description: Unicode normalization form recorded for the canonical URI. mixed_observed means the root mixes forms and the canonical URI is kept in the form the root observed.

value.description: Opaque string encoding of the identity token. Adapter code is responsible for the encoding; consumers treat it as an opaque equality-comparable handle.

strongest_identity_token.description: The strongest identity token the root can provide. Drives compare-before-write and external-change detection.

fallback_identity_tokens.description: Ordered fallback identity tokens the root guarantees.

canonical_filesystem_object.description: Layer 3: the real object the next write will target.

resolution_chain.description: Optional step-by-step resolution chain. Support bundles and alias-disclosure affordances quote this verbatim.

alias_set.description: Layer 4: the known alternative paths to the same canonical object.

capability_flags.description: Mirror of the ADR-0006 capability-flags set. Carried on every save-target token so non-VFS surfaces can explain which guarantees hold without re-querying the root.

observed_at.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp at which this generation token was observed. Consumer treats it as opaque.

compare_before_write_generation_token.description: Generation token observed at save-target-token allocation time. The save pipeline compares against a fresh read before writing.

semantic_readiness.description: Optional semantic-readiness frame attached to the identity record. Present on partially-ready workspaces and on any surface that renders a readiness chip next to the object.

filesystem_identity_record.description: The four-layer filesystem-identity record (layers 1-4 of the vocabulary). Exported by every surface that names a file.

atomic_write_mode.description: The save mode this token authorises. Derived from capability_flags at token-allocation time.

semantic_readiness.description: Optional readiness frame attached to the save-target token. Surfaces that preview apply/revert against the token render this frame; the exporter writes it verbatim.

save_target_token_record.description: Layer 5: the save-target token the editor, AI apply, review apply, or CLI pins at open time and that the save pipeline compares on write. A surface that cannot produce one MUST NOT offer a save affordance.

semantic_readiness_state.description: Frozen state set (§2.1 of the vocabulary). Only `exact` may ride with ADR-0005 freshness=authoritative.

not_ready_reason.description: Typed not-ready reason (§2.2 of the vocabulary). Required when semantic_readiness is anything other than `exact`. Support bundles and attention-center rows quote this code verbatim.

safe_next_action.description: Frozen safe-next-action set (§2.3 of the vocabulary). A not-ready surface MUST expose at least one entry.

detail_target.description: Canonical command id or deep link the chip opens. Must resolve through the command plane.

readiness_chip.description: Projection slot reserved for the compact UI chip surface. The product, CLI, and support share this record.

title.description: Short title (<= 64 graphemes) that leads the not-ready detail surface.

body.description: Longer explanation (<= 1024 graphemes) quoted verbatim by the support bundle and by assistive-tech announcements.

what_still_works.description: Narrowed capability list the user can still rely on. Aligned with the PRD local-core continuity rules.

not_ready_explainer.description: Projection slot reserved for the not-ready detail surface.

command_id.description: Canonical command id invoked by the affordance. Must resolve in the command plane.

consequence.description: One-sentence description of what invoking the action will do. Support bundles quote verbatim.

safe_next_action_row.description: Projection slot reserved for one safe-next-action row. Multiple rows may be offered, but a surface MUST expose at least one.

redaction_policy.description: Redaction policy id applied to the exported record. The policy is never overridden per-surface.

parity_signature.description: Content digest that lets the support surface and the in-product surface prove they are showing the same record.

support_export_projection.description: Projection slot reserved for support / export parity fields.

producer_id.description: Stable producer id (graph, lexical search, docs pack, type index, generator, import source).

observed_at.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp at which the readiness state was observed. Consumers treat it as opaque.

explainer.description: Optional short, redaction-aware free-text explainer (<= 240 graphemes). The product surface and the support bundle render the same explainer.

safe_next_actions.description: One or more safe-next-action rows. At least one row is required when state is not `exact`; `no_action_required` is a valid row.

semantic_readiness.description: Semantic-readiness frame (§2 of the vocabulary). Rides alongside the identity layers and the save-target token; every surface that displays, exports, or reasons about an object renders this frame verbatim.

oneOf.description: Readiness claim scoped to a single canonical filesystem object.
Readiness claim scoped to a workspace, root, or workset. Used by the partially-ready composite chip.
Readiness claim scoped to a single producer (graph, lexical search, docs pack, type index, generator). Used by producer-detail surfaces.

child_records.description: Optional nested readiness records. A workspace-scoped composite record carries one child per producer; a partially-ready workspace exports the composite plus its children so support bundles and CLI doctor flows can reconstruct the detail surface.

semantic_readiness_record.description: Standalone semantic-readiness record. Rendered by partially-ready workspace chrome, the CLI `doctor`-equivalent, support bundles, and attention-center rows. The same record ships to every surface.
